conclusion: Viable systems across all four domains occupy a common region of the 5-axis space defined by C ≥ 0.75, P ≥ 0.65, G ≥ 0.40, R ≥ 0.30, S ≥ 0.10. The 9-assumption substrate satisfies these constraints, but the constraints are not beneath the substrate — they are ABOVE it, as descriptive properties that any viable system exhibits. The substrate is a sufficient condition for satisfying them in the epistemic domain; other domains satisfy them through entirely different mechanisms. The constraints are more universal than the substrate, but not more fundamental in a generative sense.
